title: Pseudo drift checker with periodic and idle file change detection
purpose: Monitors file system changes in a project to trigger incremental scans when files are modified, using both stat-based and hash-based checks.
module_context: Exports DriftChecker interface, DriftReport type, and createDriftChecker factory function. Defines constants for default intervals and hash sampling fraction. Includes helper functions for SHA1 hashing, array shuffling, and Git file listing.

steps.content: Compute the SHA1 hash of the input buffer or string.
Return the hash as a hexadecimal string.
Create a shallow copy of the input array.
Randomly shuffle the elements in the copied array using Fisher-Yates algorithm.
Return the shuffled array.
Spawn a Git process to list all tracked files in the project.
Collect the output from the process and split it by null bytes.
Filter out any empty strings from the split results.
Return the list of file paths.

methods.name: createDriftChecker
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, db: Database, indexer: PseudoIndexer, opts: DriftCheckerOptions): DriftChecker

steps.content: Resolve the periodic interval and idle check duration from options or use defaults.
Initialize internal state variables for timers and scan status.
Build and return a drift checker object with start, stop, checkNow, and touch methods.
Retrieve the list of tracked files from Git.
Load the indexed file records from the database.
For each tracked file, check if its modification time has changed since last scan.
If a file has changed, trigger an incremental scan for that file.
Return a report of checked files and changed files.
Retrieve a sample of files from the database that are not stubs.
Randomly select a fraction of the files for hash comparison.
For each sampled file, compute its current hash and compare with the stored hash.
If a file's hash has changed, trigger an incremental scan for that file.
Return a report of checked files and changed files.
If a scan is already in progress, return an empty report.
Mark the scan as active and begin a new asynchronous check.
Perform the specified check mode: stat, hash_sample, or full.
If the check mode is 'full', perform both stat and hash_sample checks.
Return the combined results of the checks.
Reset the scan active flag and clear the current check promise.
Clear any existing idle timer.
Set a new timeout to perform a hash_sample check after the idle duration.
If the timer supports unref, call unref to prevent it from keeping the process alive.
If no timers are active, return immediately.
Reset the idle timer to delay the next hash_sample check.
If periodic timer is already active, return immediately.
Set up a periodic timer to perform stat checks at regular intervals.
If the timer supports unref, call unref to prevent it from keeping the process alive.
Set up an idle timer to perform hash_sample checks when idle.
Wait for any ongoing check to complete.
Clear the periodic timer if it is active.
Clear the idle timer if it is active.
